summary: This week in AI news, OpenAI is reportedly launching a screenless speaker and faces a trade secret lawsuit from Apple. Meanwhile, Anthropic's latest AI discovery is making waves, and Google is revamping its image search with more AI. New York has become the first state to halt construction of new data centers, citing concerns over the environmental impact of the AI-driven building boom.
insight: The increasing use of AI in various industries is leading to a surge in demand for AI infrastructure, with companies like Reflection AI signing $1 billion compute deals to access more computing power.
highlights: OpenAI researcher Miles Wang is in talks to launch an AI drug discovery startup valued at $2B
Google is facing another AI training lawsuit from major publishers over copyrighted works
New York has halted construction of new data centers for a year, citing environmental concerns

articles.title: OpenAI researcher Miles Wang in talks to launch AI drug discovery startup valued at $2B
articles.description: The funding discussions point to investor interest in applying AI to make breakthroughs in life sciences.

articles.title: OpenAI’s first hardware device is reportedly a screenless speaker that can move

articles.title: OpenAI pushes back on Apple trade secret lawsuit
articles.description: OpenAI has issued another statement on the lawsuit, this time suggesting it lacks merit.

articles.title: OpenAI may announce a ChatGPT smart speaker this year
articles.description: OpenAI's first device is set to be a smart speaker that lets you talk with ChatGPT, according to a report from Bloomberg. The device apparently won't have a screen, but will use a

articles.title: Lawsuit claims Meta's layoff decisions were made by AI, not humans
articles.description: Meta denies using AI to terminate workers with disabilities and medical problems.

articles.title: Google faces another AI training lawsuit from major publishers
articles.description: Hachette, Cengage, Elsevier, and other publishers allege that Google trained its AI on copyrighted works without the necessary permissions.

articles.title: DeepMind CEO calls for an independent standards body to regulate frontier AI
articles.description: DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis is proposing an AI "standards body" modeled after FINRA, to test frontier models and develop best practices for their release.

articles.title: Meta accused of using biased AI targeting for mass layoffs

articles.description: New York’s data center moratorium may become the blueprint for anti-AI movement.

articles.title: Apple sues OpenAI after ex-engineer allegedly used bug to steal trade secrets
articles.description: OpenAI accused of conspiring with former Apple employees to steal trade secrets.
